log.before:
You are a Frontend Developer specializing in HTML/Jinja2 templating for Flask web applications.

Your goal is to iteratively update and refine frontend templates (*.html) based on detailed feedback from frontend tests.

Task Details:
- Read frontend_feedback.txt from CONTEXT containing UI and template issues
- Update all relevant templates in templates/*.html to fix bugs and improve compliance with specifications
- Maintain consistency with OnlineAuction UI element IDs, page titles, and content structure
- Do NOT modify backend Python code or other unrelated files

Refinement Instructions:
1. Carefully analyze frontend_feedback.txt for all indicated issues including missing elements, incorrect IDs, or navigation problems.
2. Modify template files to resolve issues without adding new, unspecified features.
3. Ensure all required element IDs and page titles exactly match specifications in user task.
4. Include proper Jinja2 syntax and maintain clean, valid HTML structure.

CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS:
- Use write_text_file tool to save updated templates/*.html files
- Repeat refinement cycles until frontend_feedback.txt contains "[APPROVED]"
- Do NOT alter backend code or feedback files directly

Output: templates/*.html

log.before:
Refine and improve frontend templates based on frontend_feedback.txt if available
=== frontend_feedback.txt ===
Frontend Templates Validation Report:

1. auction_details.html
- Title matches: "Auction Details"
- Element IDs present and correct: auction-details-page, auction-title, auction-description, current-bid, place-bid-button, bid-history
- Navigation link for Place Bid button uses correct url_for with auction_id param.
- Data binding for auction and bid_history appears correct.
- No interactive filters or sorting to test here.

2. auction_status.html
- Title matches: "Auction Status"
- Element IDs present: status-page, status-filter, status-table, refresh-status-button, back-to-dashboard
- back-to-dashboard button nested correctly inside anchor linking to dashboard.
- Table headers and data binding conform to expected auction status info.

3. bid_history.html
- Title matches: "Bid History"
- Element IDs present: bid-history-page, filter-by-auction, sort-by-amount, bids-table, back-to-dashboard
- Dropdown dynamically populates auctions for filter.
- Navigation back to dashboard correct.

4. catalog.html
- Title matches: "Auction Catalog"
- Element IDs present: catalog-page, search-input, category-filter, auctions-grid
- Each auction has a view-auction-button with dynamic ID suffixed by auction_id.
- Navigation links use correct url_for references.

5. categories.html
- Title matches: "Auction Categories"
- Element IDs present: categories-page, categories-list
- Category cards contain IDs with category-card-{category_id}
- View category buttons exist per category but lack navigation links (button no link as noted).
- Navigation back to dashboard correct.

6. dashboard.html
- Title matches: "Auction Dashboard"
- Element IDs present: dashboard-page, featured-auctions
- Navigation buttons have correct IDs and point to auction_catalog, bid_history, trending_auctions routes correctly.

7. place_bid.html
- Title matches: "Place Bid"
- Element IDs present: place-bid-page, auction-name, minimum-bid, bidder-name, bid-amount, submit-bid-button
- Form action uses correct url_for with auction_id.

8. trending.html
- Title matches: "Trending Auctions"
- Element IDs present: trending-page, time-range-filter, trending-list
- Trending auctions have view-auction-button-{auction_id} buttons linking correctly.
- Back to dashboard button correct.

9. winners.html
- Title matches: "Winning Items"
- Element IDs present: winners-page, filter-by-winner, winners-list
- Winner cards have ids winner-card-{auction_id}
- Navigation back to dashboard correct.

Overall, templates meet the UI element ID and structure requirements. Navigation links and Jinja2 binding appear correct and consistent with expected routes and data.

Minor usability notes:
- categories.html's view-category-button lacks navigation links; a future route should be implemented.

No critical issues found.

[APPROVED]

log.before:
Refine frontend templates based on the following feedback:
After thoroughly testing the given frontend templates, the following detailed results are reported:

1. auction_details.html
- Page title is correct: "Auction Details".
- Body has id="auction-details-page", consistent with usage.
- The main title h1 element id="auction-title" uses {{ auction.title }} correctly.
- Navigation contains two elements linking back to dashboard:
  * <a> with id="back-to-dashboard"
  * <button> with id="dashboard-button" using onclick with location.href
  This duplication might cause usability confusion; recommend consolidating to one consistent control.
- Place bid link has id="place-bid" and uses correct url_for with auction.id.
- Bid history section and table with id="bid-history-table" uses Jinja2 iteration correctly.
- Status filter selects id="filter-status" with dynamically bound options.

2. auction_status.html
- Title "Auction Status" is correct.
- Body has a div with id="status-page", acceptable though lacking body id.
- Status filter select has id="status-filter", with only one static option; dynamic options may be expected.
- Table id="status-table" with correct column headers.
- Refresh status button id="refresh-status-button" exists.
- Back to dashboard button id="back-to-dashboard" inside anchor element.

3. bid_history.html
- Div with id="bid-history-page" as root container.
- Select dropdown id="filter-by-auction" has dynamic options.
- Sort button id="sort-by-amount" present.
- Table id="bids-table" with correct columns and dynamic data.
- Back to dashboard button exists with proper id.

4. catalog.html
- Body id="catalog-page" present.
- Each auction card has dynamic id="auction-{{ auction.id }}".
- Auction details link via url_for correct.
- Navigation anchor with id="back-to-dashboard" present.

5. categories.html
- Div id="categories-page" and categories-list div exist.
- Each category card uses dynamic id="category-card-{{ category.category_id }}".
- "View Category" buttons have ids but lack link or click event; comment notes "route not implemented" which might impair user navigation.
- Back to dashboard button with id present.

6. dashboard.html
- Body id="dashboard-page" used.
- Trending auctions listed in ul with id="trending-list".
- Each auction item has a Place Bid button without id attribute; consider adding id for potential JS interaction.
- Navigation link id="dashboard-link" points to dashboard, redundant but not erroneous.

7. place_bid.html
- Div id="place-bid-page" present.
- Auction name div id="auction-name" uses {{ auction.item_name }} (note inconsistency with auction.title in other templates).
- Minimum bid div id="minimum-bid" uses formatted currency.
- Form posts to submit_bid with correct action.
- Input elements have proper ids: "bidder-name", "bid-amount".
- Submit button id="submit-bid-button" present.

8. trending.html
- Div id="trending-page" root container.
- Time range filter select with id "time-range-filter".
- Each trending auction has a "View Auction" button with dynamic id "view-auction-button-{{ auction.auction_id }}" inside an anchor.
- Back to dashboard button with id present.

9. winners.html
- Body id="winners-page" present.
- Winner filter select id="winner-filter" with dynamic options.
- Winner cards with dynamic ids.
- Navigation button id="back-to-dashboard" present.

Summary and Recommendations:
- All required element IDs are present and used correctly.
- Minor duplication of dashboard navigation controls in auction_details.html should be streamlined.
- Missing body id in auction_status.html could be added for consistency.
- The "View Category" buttons in categories.html lack interactive navigation capability, recommend implementation.
- In dashboard.html, bid buttons lack ids for potential interaction handling.
- Variable naming inconsistency noted between auction.title and auction.item_name; backend data structure consistency should be reviewed.

No critical issues found that block functionality or navigation flow.

[APPROVED]

log.before:
Refine frontend templates based on the following feedback:
After thoroughly testing the given frontend templates, the following detailed results are reported:

1. auction_details.html
- Page title is correct: "Auction Details".
- Body has id="auction-details-page", consistent with usage.
- The main title h1 element id="auction-title" uses {{ auction.title }} correctly.
- Navigation contains two elements linking back to dashboard:
  * <a> with id="back-to-dashboard"
  * <button> with id="dashboard-button" using onclick with location.href
  This duplication might cause usability confusion; recommend consolidating to one consistent control.
- Place bid link has id="place-bid" and uses correct url_for with auction.id.
- Bid history section and table with id="bid-history-table" uses Jinja2 iteration correctly.
- Status filter selects id="filter-status" with dynamically bound options.

2. auction_status.html
- Title "Auction Status" is correct.
- Body has a div with id="status-page", acceptable though lacking body id.
- Status filter select has id="status-filter", with only one static option; dynamic options may be expected.
- Table id="status-table" with correct column headers.
- Refresh status button id="refresh-status-button" exists.
- Back to dashboard button id="back-to-dashboard" inside anchor element.

3. bid_history.html
- Div with id="bid-history-page" as root container.
- Select dropdown id="filter-by-auction" has dynamic options.
- Sort button id="sort-by-amount" present.
- Table id="bids-table" with correct columns and dynamic data.
- Back to dashboard button exists with proper id.

4. catalog.html
- Body id="catalog-page" present.
- Each auction card has dynamic id="auction-{{ auction.id }}".
- Auction details link via url_for correct.
- Navigation anchor with id="back-to-dashboard" present.

5. categories.html
- Div id="categories-page" and categories-list div exist.
- Each category card uses dynamic id="category-card-{{ category.category_id }}".
- "View Category" buttons have ids but lack link or click event; comment notes "route not implemented" which might impair user navigation.
- Back to dashboard button with id present.

6. dashboard.html
- Body id="dashboard-page" used.
- Trending auctions listed in ul with id="trending-list".
- Each auction item has a Place Bid button without id attribute; consider adding id for potential JS interaction.
- Navigation link id="dashboard-link" points to dashboard, redundant but not erroneous.

7. place_bid.html
- Div id="place-bid-page" present.
- Auction name div id="auction-name" uses {{ auction.item_name }} (note inconsistency with auction.title in other templates).
- Minimum bid div id="minimum-bid" uses formatted currency.
- Form posts to submit_bid with correct action.
- Input elements have proper ids: "bidder-name", "bid-amount".
- Submit button id="submit-bid-button" present.

8. trending.html
- Div id="trending-page" root container.
- Time range filter select with id "time-range-filter".
- Each trending auction has a "View Auction" button with dynamic id "view-auction-button-{{ auction.auction_id }}" inside an anchor.
- Back to dashboard button with id present.

9. winners.html
- Body id="winners-page" present.
- Winner filter select id="winner-filter" with dynamic options.
- Winner cards with dynamic ids.
- Navigation button id="back-to-dashboard" present.

Summary and Recommendations:
- All required element IDs are present and used correctly.
- Minor duplication of dashboard navigation controls in auction_details.html should be streamlined.
- Missing body id in auction_status.html could be added for consistency.
- The "View Category" buttons in categories.html lack interactive navigation capability, recommend implementation.
- In dashboard.html, bid buttons lack ids for potential interaction handling.
- Variable naming inconsistency noted between auction.title and auction.item_name; backend data structure consistency should be reviewed.

No critical issues found that block functionality or navigation flow.

[APPROVED]
